name: Brook Lane, Catcott ⚠ Flood
short_desc: A spacious and versatile detached house in a sought after village on The Polden Hills
full_desc:
A spacious and versatile detached house in a sought after village on The Polden Hills
Potential for a self-contained one-bedroom ground floor annexe, ideal for multi-generation living or guests
Nestled in the heart of the sought-after village of Catcott, this charming and versatile four-bedroom detached home on Brook Lane offers a unique blend of character, comfort, and flexibility. With approximately 1433 sq ft of living space, this property is ideal for families, multi-generational living, or those seeking a home with annexe potential.
Entering via the main front door, you are welcomed into the primary reception hall, with separate direct access available to the annexe if required. From here, doors lead to a cloakroom with WC and wash basin, serving the principal ground floor spaces, including a spacious sitting room with built-in bookshelves, a modern open fireplace and excellent natural light—ideal for both relaxing and entertaining year-round. The kitchen blends country charm with a modern finish, offering fitted wall and base units, a pantry, ample worktops and space for freestanding appliances. This flows into a bright, separate dining room with sliding doors opening onto the landscaped rear garden, creating an excellent space for formal dining or family gatherings. A secondary lobby, with self-contained access from the driveway, also leads to a utility room fitted with additional cabinetry, work surfaces, a drainer sink and provisions for laundry appliances. A generous second sitting room, complete with a fitted kitchenette and French doors to the garden, leads to a ground floor bedroom with a fully tiled en-suite wet room, enhanced by a tunnel light for natural daylight. This arrangement forms an ideal self-contained annexe, well-suited for guests, extended family or potential rental use. Upstairs, the first floor offers a central landing with a side-facing window and a useful storage cupboard, leading to three well-proportioned bedrooms, two with dual aspect windows. These are served by a modern bathroom with a P-shaped bath and pedestal wash basin, alongside a separate WC with an additional wash basin. Far-reaching views towards the Mendip Hills can be enjoyed from two bedrooms, the landing and cloakroom. The property has also undergone electrical upgrades within the past five years, providing added peace of mind.
A gated driveway welcomes you into this property's generous frontage, which is enclosed by stone walls and benefits from the screening of mature trees and shrubs to ensure good privacy, as well as a safe environment for families with children or pets. The substantial driveway caters for at least three cars and the remaining front garden is laid to lawn with attractive flower and shrub borders, and also benefits from an outside tap. Additional parking for two cars is available in a layby in front of the drive, owned by this property. The versatile and secluded rear garden provides a wonderful compromise between a lawned recreation space, outdoor entertaining potential and a selection of planting schemes to keep green fingered buyers occupied. It further benefits from external lighting, an outside tap and an electric socket, enhancing its practicality for year-round use. The timber garden shed also offers useful additional storage. Nature reserves can be found within approximately 1.5 and 3 miles of the property, offering excellent opportunities for walking and enjoying the surrounding countryside.

name: Main Rd, Westonzoyland ⚠ Flood
short_desc: A spacious, modern detached house with annex located in this popular and historic village which is located approximately 3½ miles from Bridgwater where all main town centre facilities & amenities are available. The village itself offers numerous facilities including convenience store, inn, primary school, community centre & church together with a r
full_desc:
A spacious, modern detached house with annex located in this popular and historic village which is located approximately 3½ miles from Bridgwater where all main town centre facilities & amenities are available. The village itself offers numerous facilities including convenience store, inn, primary school, community centre & church together with a regular bus service.
The property is constructed of cavity walling with brick and rendered elevations beneath a pitched, tiled, felted & insulated roof. A single storey extension under a pitched tiled roof to the side of the house provides a self-contained annex, ideal for those with dependant relatives. Oil fired central heating is provided together with UPVC fascias & double glazing throughout. Briefly the well-proportioned accommodation affords to the ground floor; Entrance Hall with Cloakroom off, dual aspect Lounge with French doors into the garden, Kitchen Diner with integrated appliances, whilst to the first floor there are 3 Bedrooms and Family Bathroom. The Annex, which is linked to the main accommodation but also with its own front door, provides additional accommodation including a Hall, Lounge and fully equipped Kitchen, modern Wet Room and a Double Bedroom. Outside, approached via double gates from the road there is ample parking for 3-4 vehicles, with the rear garden fully enclosed and with a high degree of privacy. To conclude the property provides excellent family size accommodation with the benefit of the annex with adaptable accommodation. Am internal inspection is thoroughly recommended in order to appreciate this property.
ENTRANCE HALL Feature staircase to first floor with storage cupboard below. Oak flooring. Radiator with cover. Inset ceiling spotlights & coving.
CLOAKROOM White suite comprising concealed cistern WC and wash hand basin. Waterproof panelling and tiling to walls. Double glazed window. Chrome towel rail/radiator.
LOUNGE 17’6” x 11’8” Fireplace recess with brick effect tiled surround and fitted woodburner and hearth, timber over mantle. Double glazed French doors to the rear garden. Double glazed front aspect window. 2 radiators. Recess for wall mounted television and shelving and cupboards under to either side. Coving. Panel glazed doors to
KITCHEN DINER 14’5” x 9’9” max Extensive range of modern matching floor & wall mounted cupboard units with sink & drainer unit with cupboards under. Integrated dishwasher & tall fridge/freezer. Range cooker with cooker hood above. Range of cupboards and drawer and wall mounted cupboards. Warmflow oil fired boiler for heating and hot water to the house and annex. 2 double glazed windows with rear garden aspect. Radiator. Coving. Door to annex hall with double glazed front door and door to the garden.
LANDING Oak handrail to the stairs, double glazed picture window, built in cupboard.
MASTER BEDROOM 15’6” x 10’5” Double glazed windows to front and rear aspects. Radiator. TV aerial point. Coving.
BEDROOM 2 18’9” x 14’6” Radiator. Double glazed window to rear. Built in double wardrobe.
BATHROOM Modern white suite comprising an L shaped panelled with shower fitting over and glazed shower screen door, low level WC, pedestal wash hand basin, part tiled and water proof panelled walls, Radiator/heated towel rail. Extractor unit. Access to part boarded roof space
THE ANNEXE Having a double-glazed door to the front with HALL also linked from the house, double glazed door to the garden and door to;
LOUNGE Double glazed French doors to the garden. Radiator, thermostat control. Opening to;
KITCHEN Range of modern units including a sink unit with cupboards under, drawer and cupboard units and wall units, cooker space, double glazed window to front.
WET ROOM Waterproof flooring, Mira Advance electric shower unit, wash hand basin, low level w.c., Velux double glazed skylight, extractor fan, radiator/towel rail.
OUTSIDE Double gates and brick boundary walling to the front with ample PARKING for several vehicles. To the rear the garden is enclosed and affords a high degree of privacy. Planted with numerous exotic plants including palm trees and ferns, there is a paved patio area and lawn, borders and wooden pergola with mature grape vine. Brick walling to the rear boundary. Garden Store/Workshop. Oil fuel tank.
Viewing By appointment with Charles Dickens Estate Agents who will be pleased to make the necessary arrangements.

Set within the heart of the West Dorset village of Chideock, Linnet Cottage is a characterful period Grade II listed thatched cottage enjoying open countryside views, generous and versatile accommodation, a large established SOUTHERLY FACING garden, PARKING and the additional benefit of a spacious SELF CONTAINED ANNEX. The location lies within easy reach of Seatown beach and the Jurassic Coast, while the surrounding countryside and nearby village amenities enhance its appeal as a main residence or lifestyle retreat.
The main cottage is arranged around a series of interconnected living spaces that retain a strong sense of character throughout. A garden room sits to the rear with double doors opening onto the terrace and garden, drawing in natural light and framing views towards the surrounding landscape. This space flows through to the kitchen, which features traditional style cabinetry, exposed stonework and a tiled floor, with room for dining. Beyond the kitchen, the sitting room provides a cosy yet spacious reception area with exposed beams, stone fireplace and a wood burning stove forming a central focal point.

name: Victoria Place, Ryde IoW ⚠ Ferry
short_desc: A characterful four-bedroom period home in a prime central Ryde setting, offering sea views, driveway parking, a generous garden with sun deck, and annex potential - blending coastal charm with flexible living.
full_desc:
A characterful four-bedroom period home in a prime central Ryde setting, offering sea views, driveway parking, a generous garden with sun deck, and annex potential - blending coastal charm with flexible living.
Dating from the early 1900s, this charming and versatile home enjoys a highly convenient position within Ryde, offering a wonderful balance of character, space, and practicality. The accommodation is thoughtfully arranged over two floors and includes a spacious sitting room, an extended kitchen which is open-plan to the dining room, and four bedrooms. A particularly appealing feature is the ground floor layout, which, with its additional bedroom, wet room, and conservatory, offers excellent potential for either guest or annex accommodation, or to be incorporated into the main home to extend the reception space. The added benefit of driveway parking is a rare advantage in this location, while the generous rear garden and sun deck provide an inviting setting for outdoor enjoyment.
Set within easy reach of Ryde’s vibrant town centre, the property benefits from a wide range of nearby amenities including shops, restaurants, schools, and transport links. The seafront and sandy beaches are just a short distance away, offering scenic walks and a relaxed coastal lifestyle. With excellent ferry connections to the mainland, this location is ideal for commuters as well as those seeking a well-connected yet coastal home.
Welcome To 1 Victoria Place - Tucked away on a quiet residential no-through-road, driveway parking leads up to the characterful façade of 1 Victoria Place. From the outset, the property sets a warm and inviting tone, with natural textures, exposed brickwork, and timber finishes creating a sense of comfort and authenticity.
Porch And Entrance Hall - The enclosed porch offers a practical space for coats and shoes, opening into a central hallway with tiled flooring and access to the principal ground floor accommodation. A door also gives access to a courtyard area.
Sitting Room - A spacious and inviting room centred around a striking exposed brick chimney breast, complemented by warm wooden flooring and a bay window that fills the space with natural light.
Dining Room - The large dining area also features a characterful chimney breast with a grand fireplace, plus a door to the rear courtyard. The dining room flows seamlessly into the kitchen, creating a sociable and functional layout ideal for both everyday living and entertaining.
Kitchen - A well-appointed kitchen featuring shaker-style cabinetry, wood-finish worktops which provide ample preparation space, neutral tiling and natural light with windows to the side aspect and a rooflight. The kitchen extends to one end, creating a fantastic prep and utility area. A door leads into the annex.
Bedroom Four/Reception Room - Located on the ground floor, this versatile room can be used as a bedroom, home office, or additional reception space, and forms part of the potential annex arrangement. A door leads to the wetroom, and French doors connect to the conservatory.
Wetroom - A convenient ground floor wetroom adds practicality with a shower, basin and WC, further supporting the potential for self-contained or multi-generational living.
Conservatory - Overlooking the garden, the conservatory provides a peaceful retreat with direct access outside, ideal as part of an annex space or as a relaxing garden room.
First-Floor Landing - A characterful turning staircase features a half-landing with a window, and leads up to a bright landing with character detailing and access to all first-floor rooms.
Bedroom One - A generous principal bedroom with a chimney breast and large bay window, enjoying elevated sea views, offering a calm and restful space with a delightful coastal outlook.
Bedrooms Two And Three - Both bedrooms are well-sized and adaptable, and both have south-facing windows to the rear aspect looking over the garden, suitable for family members, guests, or home working. Bedroom three is accessed from bedroom two, so the two could be combined to create a wonderful suite.
Family Bathroom - A centrally located bathroom serving the first floor, fitted with a large corner shower, countertop basin and WC, and finished with neutral tiling and enchanting exposed brick details.
Outside - To the front, the space is utilised to maximise the parking. To the side of the property, a courtyard is an enclosed space, ideal for outside dining or storage. A gate leads to a side path which gives access to the garden. The south-facing garden is arranged across attractive tiers, offering a mix of planting, lawn, and seating areas. A raised sun deck provides the perfect spot for entertaining or relaxing, while mature trees and established borders create a private and inviting outdoor space.
Combining character, flexibility, and a highly convenient Ryde setting, this four-bedroom home offers a rare opportunity to enjoy both accessibility and coastal living. The annex potential, driveway parking, sea views, and generous garden further enhance its appeal. An early viewing with the sole agent Susan Payne Property is highly recommended.

name: Queen Bower, Sandown IoW ⚠ Ferry
short_desc: Situated in the semi-rural Hamlet of Queen's Bower and with a direct access from the rear garden to Borthwood Copse this beautifully presented 4 Bedroom property really must be seen. Sitting on a large plot with mature gardens and to the rear and side and ample off road parking for several vehicles or motor home. We feel that subject to the necessa
full_desc:
Situated in the semi-rural Hamlet of Queen's Bower and with a direct access from the rear garden to Borthwood Copse this beautifully presented 4 Bedroom property really must be seen. Sitting on a large plot with mature gardens and to the rear and side and ample off road parking for several vehicles or motor home. We feel that subject to the necessary consent a potential plot could be created. To the rear of the garden is a superb detached Annexe which we feel could also suit someone working from home. The main property is well presented and offers spacious 4 Bedroom accommodation. The living area opens to a lovely country style Kitchen/Diner overlooking the mature gardens as does the Conservatory which is accessed from the Lounge. To fully appreciate this stunning and rare opportunity to own a property in this highly sought location, we would recommend a viewing.
Open Plan Living/Kitchen - 4.14m x 4.11m (13'7 x 13'6) - With double glazed double doors to the Terrace
Shower Room - 2.36m x 2.06m (7'9 x 6'9) - With three piece suite (connected to mains drainage)
Outside - Front: to the front of the property is a gravel driveway providing off road parking for several vehicles. Gated access to the side garden which is laid to patio which wraps around the conservatory (part of the patio is to be laid).
Rear: The mature rear garden is mainly laid to lawn with pathway leading to the Detached ANNEXE and good size Workshop. At the rear of the garden is a gate providing a direct access to Borthwood Copse providing many miles of stunning woodland walks.

full_desc: Three separate buildings on 6.5 acres. One building move-in ready (EPC C). Two unconverted agricultural buildings with planning potential. Nine bedrooms total across complex. Carmarthen 8 miles, coast accessible. The proposition is to live in the finished building as annexe while pursuing residential conversion consent for one of the barns as the main house — or vice versa. Welsh rural planning is complex; Carmarthenshire County Council pre-application advice essential. Budget minimum £80k-150k for barn conversion on top of purchase price. Guide price £465k leaves almost nothing for works. Exceptional potential if planning succeeds.

full_desc: GUIDE PRICE £400,000–£425,000. Substantial detached family home in Alltwen, Pontardawe SA8. Approximately 2,066 sq ft over three floors.
